column1: Question Bank ó Extended with Analytical Metadata (Methodology Section 2)

column5: KPI
column6: Question
column8: Response Scale
column14: Notes

column5: % villages with a functioning primary health facility within 5km
column6: Is there a functioning health facility (clinic/health center) within 5 km of this village?
column8: Yes / No / Don't know
column14: Core access indicator; anchors all downstream health KPIs.

column5: Average distance to nearest functioning health facility (km)
column6: What is the approximate distance (in km) from the village center to the nearest functioning health facility?
column8: Open numeric (km)
column14: Distinct from binary access ó captures gradient of physical access for GIS mapping.

column5: % of year the nearest facility is operational (not closed/understaffed)
column6: During the past 12 months, was the nearest health facility closed or unstaffed for more than 2 consecutive weeks at any point?
column8: Yes / No / Don't know
column14: Captures reliability dimension distinct from existence of facility.

column5: % households reporting transport as a barrier to reaching care
column6: Does lack of transportation prevent household members from reaching health services when needed?
column8: Always / Often / Sometimes / Never
column14: Separates access barrier type (transport) from distance ó actionable for different interventions.

column5: % pregnant women receiving ?4 antenatal visits
column6: Among women in this household who were pregnant in the past 2 years, did they receive at least 4 antenatal check-ups?
column8: Yes, all / Some did / None did / Not applicable
column14: WHO-aligned ANC4 standard; key maternal health KPI.

column5: % births attended by a skilled health professional
column6: Was the most recent birth in this household attended by a doctor, nurse, or trained midwife?
column8: Yes / No / Not applicable
column14: SDG 3.1 indicator proxy.

column5: % children under 5 with complete vaccination schedule
column6: Have all children under 5 in this household received their complete scheduled vaccinations?
column8: Yes, all / Partially / None / No children under 5
column14: Standard EPI coverage proxy.

column5: % children under 5 with regular growth/nutrition monitoring
column6: Are children under 5 in this household regularly weighed and monitored for growth at a health facility?
column8: Yes, regularly / Occasionally / Never / No children under 5
column14: Early warning indicator for malnutrition risk.

column5: % households with at least one member with a diagnosed chronic condition
column6: Does any member of this household have a diagnosed chronic condition (diabetes, hypertension, etc.)?
column8: Yes / No / Don't know
column14: Demand-side signal for service planning, not a quality measure.

column5: % chronically ill members able to access regular follow-up care
column6: If yes, can this household member access regular follow-up care/medication for this condition locally?
column8: Yes, easily / Yes, with difficulty / No / Not applicable
column14: Conditional follow-up ó measures continuity of care gap.

column5: % households aware of available mental health support channels
column6: Do you know where to seek help if someone in your household needed mental health or psychosocial support?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Awareness precedes utilization; foundational indicator.

column5: Level of perceived community stigma toward seeking mental health support
column6: How comfortable would people in this community be seeking mental health support openly?
column8: Very uncomfortable / Uncomfortable / Neutral / Comfortable / Very comfortable
column14: Captures social/cultural barrier distinct from physical access.

column5: % households rating health service quality as adequate or above
column6: How would you rate the overall quality of health services available to this village?
column8: Very poor / Poor / Fair / Good / Very good
column14: Subjective quality complement to objective availability indicators.

column5: % households reporting consistent availability of essential medicines
column6: When you visit the local health facility, are essential/prescribed medicines usually available?
column8: Always / Usually / Rarely / Never
column14: Supply-chain proxy at point of care.

column5: % households reporting health costs as a barrier to seeking care
column6: Has the cost of healthcare (consultation, medicine, transport) prevented this household from seeking needed care in the past year?
column8: Yes, often / Yes, sometimes / No
column14: Affordability dimension distinct from physical/geographic access.

column5: Perceived adequacy of emergency medical response time
column6: If a medical emergency occurred, how long would it typically take for help (ambulance/transport to facility) to arrive or be reached?
column8: Under 30 min / 30ñ60 min / 1ñ2 hours / Over 2 hours / No emergency service available
column14: Captures a distinct readiness dimension from routine care access.

column5: % school-age children (6ñ15) currently enrolled in school
column6: Are all school-age children (6ñ15 years) in this household currently enrolled in school?
column8: Yes, all / Some / None / No school-age children
column14: Core enrollment indicator (SDG 4.1 proxy).

column5: Average distance to nearest primary school (km)
column6: What is the approximate distance from this household to the nearest primary school?
column8: Open numeric (km)
column14: Physical access driver for enrollment and dropout.

column5: % households reporting safe and reliable transport to school
column6: Do children in this household have safe and reliable transportation to and from school?
column8: Yes / Partially / No / Not applicable
column14: Distinguishes safety/reliability from mere distance.

column5: % households reporting a child who dropped out in the past 2 years
column6: Has any school-age child in this household stopped attending school in the past 2 years?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Direct dropout indicator; pairs with reasons question below.

column5: Most cited reasons for school dropout
column6: If yes, what was the main reason for stopping school? (financial / distance / safety / early marriage / work / disability / other)
column8: Financial / Distance / Safety / Early marriage / Work / Disability / Academic difficulty / Other
column14: Root-cause classification ó feeds targeted intervention design; only shown if dropout = Yes.

column5: Ratio of girls to boys enrolled (school-age)
column6: Are there any school-age girls in this household NOT enrolled in school while boys of similar age are enrolled?
column8: Yes / No / Not applicable
column14: Direct gender-parity gap detector at household level.

column5: % households perceiving specific barriers to girls' education
column6: What, if any, barriers do girls in this village face in continuing their education that boys do not?
column8: Distance/safety / Lack of female teachers / Early marriage / Household duties / Cultural norms / None
column14: Qualitative-to-quantitative barrier classification.

column5: % parents satisfied with quality of teaching at local school
column6: How satisfied are you with the quality of teaching at the school your children attend?
column8: Very dissatisfied / Dissatisfied / Neutral / Satisfied / Very satisfied
column14: Demand-side quality perception, complements infrastructure indicators.

column5: % schools perceived to have adequate teaching staff
column6: Does the local school have enough teachers to cover all subjects/grades?
column8: Yes / Partially / No / Don't know
column14: Supply-side staffing adequacy.

column5: % households reporting children have adequate textbooks/learning materials
column6: Do your children have access to the textbooks and learning materials they need for their grade level?
column8: Yes, fully / Partially / No
column14: Material adequacy distinct from teacher/infrastructure quality.

column5: % schools with adequate classrooms, water, and sanitation
column6: Does the school your children attend have adequate classrooms, clean water, and functioning toilets?
column8: Yes, all adequate / Some inadequate / Most inadequate / Don't know
column14: Bundles core infrastructure adequacy; can be split later if granularity needed.

column5: % schools with functioning computer/internet access for students
column6: Does the school have functioning computers or internet access available for student use?
column8: Yes / No / Don't know
column14: Digital divide indicator relevant to SDG 4.4 (digital skills).

column5: % youth aware of and able to access vocational/technical training
column6: Are vocational or technical training programs available and accessible to youth in this village?
column8: Yes, accessible / Exist but inaccessible / None available / Don't know
column14: Bridges education domain to livelihood/employment domain.

column5: % adult household members unable to read/write functionally
column6: Are there adult members of this household who cannot read or write?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Adult literacy gap, SDG 4.6 proxy.

column5: % households with access to an improved drinking water source
column6: What is the main source of drinking water for this household?
column8: Piped into dwelling / Public tap / Protected well / Unprotected well / Surface water (river/pond) / Tanker/vendor / Other
column14: JMP/WHO improved-source classification; foundational SDG 6.1 indicator.

column5: Average round-trip time to collect water (minutes)
column6: How long does it take (round trip) to collect water from the main source, if not piped to the home?
column8: Water on premises / Under 30 min / 30 minñ1 hr / Over 1 hr
column14: JMP standard threshold (30 min) for 'basic' service; gendered time-burden proxy.

column5: % households reporting water source available year-round
column6: Is your main water source available consistently throughout the year, including dry season?
column8: Yes, consistently / Seasonal shortages / Frequent shortages / Rarely available
column14: Captures reliability/seasonality distinct from source type.

column5: % households perceiving their drinking water as safe to drink
column6: Do you consider your household's drinking water safe to drink without treatment?
column8: Yes / No, needs treatment / Don't know
column14: Subjective safety perception; pairs with treatment practice question.

column5: % households practicing water treatment before drinking
column6: Does this household treat water (boiling, filtering, chlorination) before drinking it?
column8: Always / Sometimes / Never
column14: Behavioral mitigation indicator.

column5: % households reporting waterborne illness in past 6 months
column6: Has any household member experienced illness suspected to be related to water quality in the past 6 months (e.g., diarrhea)?
column8: Yes / No / Don't know
column14: Outcome-level proxy for water safety problems.

column5: % households with access to an improved sanitation facility
column6: What type of toilet facility does this household primarily use?
column8: Flush/pour-flush to sewer or septic / Pit latrine with slab / Pit latrine without slab / Shared/public facility / Open defecation
column14: JMP improved-sanitation classification; SDG 6.2 core indicator.

column5: % households reporting sanitation facility is private and safe to use, especially at night
column6: Is your household's toilet facility private and safe to use, including at night?
column8: Yes / Somewhat / No
column14: Critical gender/safety dimension often missed in basic access counts.

column5: % households with a handwashing facility with water and soap
column6: Does this household have a handwashing facility with both water and soap available?
column8: Yes / Water only, no soap / No facility
column14: SDG 6.2 hygiene sub-indicator.

column5: % villages with regular solid waste collection service
column6: Is there a regular waste collection service operating in this village?
column8: Yes, weekly or more / Yes, irregular / No service
column14: Service coverage indicator.

column5: Predominant method of waste disposal in absence of collection service
column6: In the absence of formal collection, how does this household primarily dispose of waste?
column8: Burning / Burying / Open dumping / Private collection / Other
column14: Only relevant where collection = No/irregular; environmental risk proxy.

column5: % households connected to the national electricity grid
column6: Is this household connected to the national electricity grid?
column8: Yes / No, uses alternative source / No electricity access
column14: SDG 7.1 core access indicator.

column5: Average daily hours of electricity outage
column6: On a typical day, how many hours does the electricity supply get interrupted?
column8: No outages / Under 2 hrs / 2ñ6 hrs / Over 6 hrs / No grid connection
column14: Reliability dimension distinct from binary connectivity.

column5: % households relying on alternative/off-grid energy sources
column6: If not connected to the grid, what is the household's main energy source?
column8: Solar / Generator / Gas / Firewood/biomass / Other / Not applicable
column14: Identifies feasibility of off-grid renewable interventions.

column5: % households using clean cooking fuel/technology
column6: What is the primary fuel or technology this household uses for cooking?
column8: Electricity/LPG/natural gas / Improved cookstove / Firewood/charcoal/open fire / Other
column14: SDG 7.1.2 clean cooking access indicator; health and environment co-benefit.

column5: % households reporting health concerns from cooking smoke
column6: Do household members experience respiratory or eye irritation symptoms believed to be related to cooking smoke?
column8: Yes, frequently / Occasionally / Never / Not applicable
column14: Health-environment linkage indicator.

column5: % households reporting seasonal water scarcity affecting agriculture/livestock
column6: Does this household experience water scarcity that affects farming or livestock during part of the year?
column8: Yes, severe / Yes, moderate / No
column14: Links environmental stress to livelihood domain.

column5: Perceived change in land/soil quality over past 5 years
column6: Compared to 5 years ago, how would you describe the quality of agricultural land/soil in this area?
column8: Improved / Same / Degraded / Don't know
column14: Trend-based perceptual environmental indicator.

column5: % households affected by a natural hazard event in past 3 years
column6: Has this household been affected by a flood, drought, or other natural hazard in the past 3 years?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Climate vulnerability/resilience baseline.

column5: % households aware of environmental or clean energy programs available locally
column6: Are you aware of any environmental conservation or clean energy programs operating in or near this village?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Awareness precedes uptake; baseline for outreach planning.

column5: % households practicing any form of water/resource conservation
column6: Does this household practice any form of water conservation or resource-saving measures?
column8: Yes, regularly / Occasionally / No
column14: Behavioral adoption indicator.

column5: % working-age members (15-64) currently employed or self-employed
column6: What is the current employment status of the primary working-age members of this household?
column8: Employed full-time / Employed part-time / Self-employed / Seeking work / Not seeking work / Unable to work
column14: Core labor-market status indicator (SDG 8.5 proxy).

column5: % households reporting income sufficient to cover basic needs
column6: Does this household's income cover its basic needs (food, housing, health, education)?
column8: Comfortably covers / Barely covers / Does not cover
column14: Subjective income-adequacy; complements objective poverty-line data where unavailable.

column5: Stability/seasonality of household's main income source
column6: How stable is the household's main source of income throughout the year?
column8: Stable year-round / Seasonal, predictable / Irregular/unpredictable
column14: Differentiates chronic poverty from seasonal vulnerability.

column5: Number of distinct income sources for the household
column6: How many different sources of income does this household currently rely on?
column8: One / Two / Three or more / None
column14: Resilience proxy ó diversified income reduces shock vulnerability.

column5: % households dependent on agriculture/livestock as primary livelihood
column6: Is agriculture, livestock, or fishing the primary source of livelihood for this household?
column8: Yes, primary / Yes, secondary / No
column14: Sector-dependency baseline for targeting agri-interventions.

column5: % agricultural households reporting adequate access to inputs (seeds, feed, fertilizer) and output markets
column6: Does this household have adequate access to agricultural inputs and a market to sell its produce/livestock?
column8: Yes, both / Inputs only / Market only / Neither / Not applicable
column14: Value-chain bottleneck identification; only relevant to agri-dependent households.

column5: Most cited constraint limiting agricultural productivity
column6: What is the single biggest constraint limiting agricultural/livestock productivity for this household?
column8: Water/irrigation / Land size / Inputs cost / Market access / Climate/weather / Lack of equipment / Lack of skills / Other
column14: Root-cause classification for targeted productivity interventions.

column5: % households operating a small business or microenterprise
column6: Does this household currently own or operate a small business or microenterprise?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Baseline entrepreneurial activity rate.

column5: % non-business households expressing intent to start a business
column6: If not currently operating a business, would members of this household like to start one?
column8: Yes, with a clear idea / Yes, but no clear idea / No interest / Not applicable
column14: Demand-side signal for entrepreneurship support programs.

column5: Most cited barrier to starting/growing a business
column6: What is the main barrier preventing this household from starting or growing a business?
column8: Lack of capital / Lack of skills/training / Lack of market / Regulatory/permit issues / Lack of mentorship / Other
column14: Feeds design of targeted capacity-building or financing interventions.

column5: % households with access to formal or microfinance credit
column6: Does this household have access to a bank account, microfinance, or formal credit when needed?
column8: Yes, easily / Yes, with difficulty / No access
column14: Financial-inclusion indicator (SDG 8.10 related).

column5: % working-age members holding a marketable vocational/technical skill
column6: Do working-age members of this household hold any vocational or technical skill (e.g., carpentry, tailoring, repair trades)?
column8: Yes / No / Don't know
column14: Human-capital baseline distinct from formal education attainment.

column5: % working-age members who participated in a skills/training program in the past 2 years
column6: Have any working-age members of this household participated in a job-training or skills program in the past 2 years?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Program reach indicator.

column5: % women in household employed, self-employed, or actively seeking work
column6: Are the women in this household employed, self-employed, or actively seeking paid work?
column8: Yes / No, by choice / No, due to barriers / Not applicable
column14: SDG 5/8 intersection; distinguishes voluntary from constrained non-participation.

column5: Main barrier to women's economic participation, where applicable
column6: If women in this household face barriers to working, what is the primary barrier?
column8: Childcare/household duties / Cultural/social norms / Lack of nearby opportunities / Transportation / Lack of skills / Not applicable
column14: Root-cause classification specific to gendered economic exclusion.

column5: Do women in the household have control/decision-making over income they earn
column6: If women in this household earn income, do they have control over how it is spent or saved?
column8: Full control / Shared decision / Limited control / Not applicable
column14: Empowerment depth indicator beyond mere participation.

column5: % villages connected by an all-weather paved or graded road
column6: Is this village connected to the main road network by a paved or all-weather road?
column8: Yes, paved / Yes, graded/unpaved but usable / No, often impassable
column14: Core connectivity indicator affecting access to all other services.

column5: % households with access to regular public/shared transport
column6: Is regular public or shared transportation available connecting this village to nearby towns/cities?
column8: Yes, daily / Yes, limited schedule / No regular service
column14: Mobility access distinct from road quality ó affects market/service access.

column5: % of year the village is fully accessible by road
column6: Are there times of the year (e.g., rainy season) when this village becomes difficult or impossible to reach by road?
column8: Always accessible / Occasionally difficult / Frequently inaccessible
column14: Seasonal vulnerability indicator critical for service delivery planning.

column5: % households with reliable mobile network coverage
column6: Is mobile phone network coverage reliable at this household's location?
column8: Yes, strong signal / Weak/intermittent signal / No coverage
column14: Foundational connectivity layer, prerequisite for digital services.

column5: % households with functioning internet access (mobile data or fixed)
column6: Does this household have access to functioning internet, through mobile data or a fixed connection?
column8: Yes, reliable / Yes, but unreliable/slow / No access
column14: SDG 9.c digital-divide indicator.

column5: % household members able to use a smartphone/internet for basic tasks
column6: Can members of this household use a smartphone or internet for basic tasks (e.g., messaging, browsing, mobile banking)?
column8: Yes, most members / Some members / No members
column14: Usability gap distinct from mere connectivity availability.

column5: % households living in structurally adequate housing
column6: Is the household's dwelling structurally adequate (sound roof, walls, protection from weather)?
column8: Yes, fully adequate / Partially adequate / Inadequate
column14: Basic shelter adequacy, related to SDG 11.1.

column5: Average number of persons per room in the household
column6: On average, how many people share each room used for sleeping in this household?
column8: Open numeric (persons/room)
column14: Standard overcrowding metric (UN-Habitat threshold: 3+ persons/room = crowded).

column5: % households within reasonable distance of a functioning market
column6: Is there a functioning market (for buying/selling goods) within reasonable distance of this village?
column8: Yes, within village / Yes, nearby (under 10km) / No, far (over 10km)
column14: Economic-infrastructure linkage supporting livelihood domain.

column5: Availability of a functioning community center or public gathering space
column6: Does this village have a functioning community center, public square, or gathering space?
column8: Yes, well-maintained / Yes, poorly maintained / No
column14: Social infrastructure baseline supporting social cohesion domain.

column5: % households feeling safe in their village, day and night
column6: How safe do members of this household feel walking in this village, both during the day and at night?
column8: Very unsafe / Unsafe / Neutral / Safe / Very safe
column14: Composite safety perception across day/night conditions.

column5: % vulnerable households with access to a social protection program
column6: Does this household currently receive support from any social protection or welfare program?
column8: Yes / No, but eligible / No, not eligible / Don't know eligibility
column14: Social protection coverage gap indicator (SDG 1.3).

column5: % elderly household members with access to adequate care/support services
column6: Do elderly members of this household have access to adequate care, support, or social services?
column8: Yes / Partially / No / Not applicable
column14: Targets aging population needs, often under-surveyed.

column5: % public facilities in the village accessible to persons with disabilities
column6: Are public facilities in this village (schools, health centers, public buildings) accessible to persons with disabilities?
column8: Yes, mostly / Partially / Not accessible / No persons with disabilities in household
column14: Inclusion indicator (SDG 10) often absent from generic needs surveys.

column5: % households with a member having a disability affecting daily function
column6: Does any member of this household have a disability that affects their daily activities?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Prevalence baseline enabling targeted service planning.

column5: % households aware of how to report child abuse or neglect
column6: Do you know how or where to report a case of child abuse or neglect in this area?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Protection-system awareness gap indicator.

column5: % youth (15-24) participating in community or volunteer activities
column6: Are youth (ages 15-24) in this household engaged in any community, volunteer, or civic activities?
column8: Yes, actively / Occasionally / Not engaged
column14: Social capital and youth inclusion proxy.

column5: % households with a member married before age 18
column6: Has any member of this household been married before the age of 18?
column8: Yes / No / Prefer not to answer
column14: Sensitive but critical SDG 5.3 indicator; requires careful field protocol.

column5: % households reporting strong sense of belonging/trust within the village
column6: How strong is your household's sense of belonging and trust within this village community?
column8: Very weak / Weak / Moderate / Strong / Very strong
column14: Core social-capital indicator underpinning collective action capacity.

column5: % households reporting unresolved social or land/resource conflict in the past year
column6: Has this household been involved in or affected by an unresolved community, land, or resource conflict in the past year?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Stability/tension indicator relevant to intervention sequencing.

column5: % villages with an active community-led group/committee addressing local issues
column6: Is there an active community group, council, or committee in this village that addresses local needs?
column8: Yes, active / Exists but inactive / None
column14: Organizational readiness for participatory development approaches.

column5: % villages with identifiable tangible or intangible cultural heritage assets
column6: Does this village have cultural heritage sites, crafts, or traditions that are actively recognized or preserved?
column8: Yes, actively preserved / Exist but undocumented/at risk / None identified
column14: Baseline asset-mapping indicator for cultural-economy and identity programs.

column5: Perceived risk of loss/erosion of local cultural practices or sites
column6: Do you feel that local traditions, crafts, or heritage sites are at risk of being lost in the coming generation?
column8: High risk / Some risk / Low risk / Don't know
column14: Forward-looking risk indicator distinct from current-state presence.

column5: % households aware of cultural, arts, or heritage programs available locally
column6: Are there any cultural, arts, or heritage programs/events available in or near this village?
column8: Yes / No / Don't know
column14: Program-reach baseline.

column5: % households that participated in a local cultural or heritage activity in the past year
column6: Has any member of this household participated in a local cultural, heritage, or arts activity in the past 12 months?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Engagement/uptake indicator distinct from mere availability.

column5: % households expressing pride in local cultural identity/heritage
column6: How proud do members of this household feel about the village's cultural identity and heritage?
column8: Not at all proud / Slightly proud / Moderately proud / Proud / Very proud
column14: Identity-strength indicator distinct from Social Development's general belonging metric ó culturally specific.

column5: % households where traditional skills/knowledge are being passed to younger generations
column6: Are traditional skills, crafts, or knowledge being actively taught to younger generations in this household/community?
column8: Yes, actively / Somewhat / Not happening
column14: Sustainability-of-heritage indicator with practical succession implications.

column5: % households reporting easy access to government service centers
column6: How easy is it for this household to access government service centers (civil registry, licensing, etc.) when needed?
column8: Very difficult / Difficult / Moderate / Easy / Very easy
column14: Core institutional-access indicator.

column5: % household members with complete civil documentation (ID, birth registration)
column6: Do all members of this household have complete civil documentation (national ID, birth certificate, etc.)?
column8: Yes, all / Some missing / Don't know
column14: SDG 16.9 legal identity indicator ó foundational for accessing all other services.

column5: % households that have used at least one e-government service in the past 6 months
column6: Has this household used any government digital/e-service (e.g., online applications, digital ID platforms) in the past 6 months?
column8: Yes / No, but aware / No, unaware of options
column14: Adoption indicator distinct from awareness ó feeds digital-inclusion strategy.

column5: % households satisfied with the e-government services they used
column6: If used, how satisfied were you with the e-government service experience?
column8: Very dissatisfied / Dissatisfied / Neutral / Satisfied / Very satisfied / Not applicable
column14: Quality-of-experience indicator conditional on usage.

column5: % adults who participate in any local committee, council, or association
column6: Do adult members of this household participate in any local committee, council, or community association?
column8: Yes, actively / Occasionally / No
column14: Civic-engagement indicator, distinct from the Social Cohesion 'collective capacity' indicator ó this is individual participation, not group existence.

column5: Perceived ability of residents to influence decisions affecting the village
column6: Do residents of this village feel they have a real opportunity to influence decisions that affect the village?
column8: No influence at all / Limited influence / Moderate influence / Strong influence / Very strong influence
column14: Empowerment/voice indicator central to participatory development (SDG 16.7).

column5: Level of trust in local governing/administrative bodies
column6: How much do you trust the local governing or administrative bodies serving this village?
column8: No trust / Low trust / Moderate trust / High trust / Very high trust
column14: Institutional-trust baseline; sensitive but important for legitimacy of interventions.

column5: % households aware of a channel to file complaints/grievances about public services
column6: Do you know of a formal channel to file a complaint or grievance about public services in this area?
column8: Yes / No
column14: Accountability-mechanism awareness gap indicator.
